node.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Garden Veg Combo
node.short_description: Homemade garden-veggie pizza with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper—colorful and fiber-rich.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve a personal 8–12 inch pizza with a side salad. For a lighter version, use part-skim mozzarella and low-sodium tomato sauce, and consider adding more vegetables or a lean protein if desired.
node.nutrition_note: Balanced: whole-wheat crust (fiber) + mozzarella (protein/calcium) + veggies (vitamins/minerals). Carbs mainly from crust; monitor sodium from cheese/sauce; consider portion control and optional low-sodium ingredients or extra veggies for fiber.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 4 > Child > Day 3 > Dinner > Roast Chicken Dinner — Sesame Bake

node.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Sesame Bake
node.short_description: Roast chicken thighs with quinoa, broccoli, and carrots in a sesame style bake for a protein-rich, veggie-forward dinner.
node.meal_suggestion: Great for a satisfying, make ahead dinner. For extra flavor, consider topping with sesame seeds before roasting. Serve with a simple side salad to boost greens.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-dense meal from chicken thighs and quinoa; includes fiber from quinoa and vegetables. Provides vitamins A and C from broccoli and carrots, plus minerals like iron and potassium. Moderate fat from chicken thighs; balanced carbohydrates from quinoa and vegetables; low in added sugars.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 4 > Child > Day 6 > Dinner > Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Wrap

node.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Wrap
node.short_description: Tofu with mixed vegetables in curry sauce served with rice; a protein-rich, veggie-forward plant-based meal with a citrus-inspired fresh note.
node.meal_suggestion: To boost fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa. Opt for a low-sodium curry sauce and add a side of leafy greens for extra volume and micronutrients. A squeeze of lemon or lime can enhance the citrus freshness mentioned in the name.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich tofu provides plant-based protein; fiber from vegetables and rice supports fullness. Carbohydrates mainly come from rice. Depending on the curry sauce, sodium and added sugars may be high; check for dairy and gluten content if you have sensitivities.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 4 > Child > Day 6 > Dinner > Baked Cod Plate — Lemon-Herb Skillet

node.name: Baked Cod Plate — Lemon-Herb Skillet
node.short_description: Baked cod with lemon-herb flavor, served with roasted sweet potato and broccoli for a light, balanced meal.
node.meal_suggestion: Bake cod and vegetables in a lemon-herb skillet; 25–30 minutes total. Pair with a small portion of quinoa or brown rice for extra whole grains, and a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
node.nutrition_note: Lean cod provides high-quality protein with low fat; sweet potato and broccoli add fiber, vitamins A, C, and K, and minerals. Lemon adds vitamin C and fresh flavor. Balanced plate with protein, complex carbs, and vegetables.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 4 > Child > Day 1 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Mild-Spice Style

node.name: DIY Taco Night — Mild-Spice Style
node.short_description: A customizable DIY taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa with mild spice.
node.meal_suggestion: Cook lean ground beef with mild taco seasoning; warm corn tortillas; assemble with lettuce, tomato, cheddar, and salsa. For extra fiber and fats, add beans or avocado. Typical portion: 1–2 tortillas, ~4 oz beef, generous veggies, 1/4 cup cheese.
node.nutrition_note: Lean beef provides high-quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates and some fiber; lettuce and tomato add vitamins and additional fiber; cheddar contributes calcium and fat. Monitor sodium from salsa and seasoning—use low-sodium salsa and moderate cheese, and consider adding beans or avocado to boost fiber and heart-healthy fats.
